name: Pylon
one_liner: B2B customer support and helpdesk platform with a REST API for programmatically managing issues, accounts, contacts, users, teams, and knowledge bases.
access_notes: API tokens can only be created by Admin users of an existing Pylon account. Pylon is a paid B2B SaaS platform with no documented free developer tier or public sandbox; a paid account is required to obtain working API credentials. The MCP server offers a free usage tier but still requires a Pylon account authenticated via OAuth.
api_notes: REST API at api.usepylon.com covering Accounts, Contacts, Issues (create/fetch), Users, Teams, Knowledge Base, and more. Authentication uses Authorization: Bearer <token> (bearerAuth http bearer scheme). Rate limited to 60 requests/minute. Roughly 20-100 operations across resources.
mcp_notes: Pylon ships an official remote MCP server at https://mcp.usepylon.com (Streamable HTTP, stateless), authenticated via OAuth 2.0 (AuthKit). Exposes read/write tools for issues, accounts, contacts, and users, scoped to the authenticated user's dashboard permissions. Documented at docs.usepylon.com/pylon-docs/integrations/pylon-mcp. Community MCP servers also exist (e.g. marcinwyszynski/pylon-mcp, JustinBeckwith/pylon-mcp).
main_blocker: API access requires a paid Pylon account and Admin privileges to mint tokens; no free/self-serve developer tier or public sandbox is documented.

evidence.claim: Only Admin users can create API tokens; API used to fetch customer/issue data and create issues.
Accounts endpoint uses Authorization: Bearer <token> (bearerAuth http bearer security scheme).
Official Pylon MCP server at https://mcp.usepylon.com uses OAuth 2.0 and exposes read/write access to issues, accounts, contacts, and users.

open_questions: Exact webhook configuration/event coverage was not fully verified from a webhooks doc page. Whether any time-limited free trial grants instant API credentials (vs. strictly paid) is not explicitly documented.
